subject: Nameserver issue fixed
commit_message:

Change the Vagrant Box image for libvirt

Issue-ID: MULTICLOUD-1004
Signed-off-by: Yao Le <le.yao@intel.com>

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

As discussed in the meeting today the issue should be solved using vagrant dns plugin.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> As discussed in the meeting today the issue should be solved using vagrant dns plugin.

Hi, but it will increase deployment complexity and require vagrant dns installation on host. And the image is downloaded from vagrang cloud hub, which is consistent with the present. ALL tests are passed both inside proxy and outside proxy.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> As discussed in the meeting today the issue should be solved using vagrant dns plugin.

In addition, when execute 'vagrant plugin install vagrant-dns', it will make conflicts with fog-core and vagrant-libvirt. It need more dependencies.

Why not using the new Vagrant Box, Which is more flexible and faster.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> As discussed in the meeting today the issue should be solved using vagrant dns plugin.

I remember that we didn't get the conclution to use vagrant dns plugin. What do you think is the advantage of using this plugin? We use libvirt as a provider to setup vm, which seems doesn't support libvirt. Any ideas? Thanks.

Patch Set 1:

> > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
 > >
 > > As discussed in the meeting today the issue should be solved
 > using vagrant dns plugin.

 > I remember that we didn't get the conclution to use vagrant dns
 > plugin. What do you think is the advantage of using this plugin? We
 > use libvirt as a provider to setup vm, which seems doesn't support
 > libvirt. Any ideas? Thanks.

In the meeting, it was mentioned that the new Ubutu image created had hardcoded IP addresses. Is that correct? Can you share what changes were made to the image?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
>  > >
>  > > As discussed in the meeting today the issue should be solved
>  > using vagrant dns plugin.
>  > 
>  > I remember that we didn't get the conclution to use vagrant dns
>  > plugin. What do you think is the advantage of using this plugin? We
>  > use libvirt as a provider to setup vm, which seems doesn't support
>  > libvirt. Any ideas? Thanks.
> 
> In the meeting, it was mentioned that the new Ubutu image created had hardcoded IP addresses. Is that correct? Can you share what changes were made to the image?

What I do are same with what the tool resolvconf do. I found the subnet IP to these VMs is 192.168.121.* in Vagrantfile. So I just add the 192.168.121.1 do the /etc/resolv.conf and remove the link to /run/systemd/resolve/stub-resolv.conf. Although it is hardcode, but we can config it through the Vagrant provision shell scripts after the NFS installation finfished.

And vagrant-dns-plugin is not support for linux. It only works on MAC OS X. https://github.com/BerlinVagrant/vagrant-dns/blob/master/PLATFORM_SUPPORT.md

Hi, Ritu
Vagrant dns plugin only supports MAC OS X system. Any idea for that? Thanks.
